description_en: A stone monument inscribed with the precepts of Takano Tomokane, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts, old stone monument). It is town-designated by Zaō.
The kagura sacred dance of Yakumo Shrine, classified as a folk cultural property (intangible folk cultural property, folk performing art). It is town-designated by Zaō.
The kagura sacred dance of Hakusan Shrine, classified as a folk cultural property (intangible folk cultural property, folk performing art). It is town-designated by Zaō.
The Higashine kagura sacred dance of the Sakaki school, classified as a folk cultural property (intangible folk cultural property, folk performing art). It is town-designated by Zaō.
The Sakaki-school kagura sacred dance of Hirasawa, classified as a folk cultural property (intangible folk cultural property, folk performing art). It is town-designated by Zaō.
The kagura sacred dance of Kattamine Shrine, classified as a folk cultural property (intangible folk cultural property, folk performing art). It is town-designated by Zaō.
The Sakaki-school hōin kagura sacred dance of Komurasaki, classified as a folk cultural property (intangible folk cultural property, folk performing art). It is town-designated by Zaō.
The taue odori (rice-planting dance) of Komurasaki, classified as a folk cultural property (intangible folk cultural property, folk performing art). It is town-designated by Zaō.
The harugoma dance of Komurasaki, classified as a folk cultural property (intangible folk cultural property, folk performing art). It is town-designated by Zaō.

name_romaji: Daruma-kō sekizōbutsu (tamaishi, miyajuku tamago, anzan Daruma-son)
name_en: Daruma-kō Stone Objects (Round Stone, Miyajuku Egg, and Safe-Childbirth Daruma Deity)
description_en: Stone objects of the Daruma-kō association, comprising a round stone, the Miyajuku egg and the safe-childbirth Daruma deity, classified as a folk cultural property (tangible folk cultural property, faith-related). It is town-designated by Zaō.
The Shirakuzuryū burial mound, classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated by Zaō.
The site of the Iwasakiyama gold mine, classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated by Zaō.

name_en: Magetake Milestone Mound, with Old Stone Monuments
description_en: The Magetake ichirizuka (milestone mound), together with an associated group of old stone monuments, classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated by Zaō.

name_en: Anyōji Temple Approach-Path Site Preservation District
description_en: The preservation district of the former approach path to Anyōji temple, classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated by Zaō.

name_en: Blast Furnace Site of the Tōgatta Ironworks
description_en: The blast furnace site of the Tōgatta ironworks, classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated by Zaō.
The Nekaeshi cherry tree, classified as a monument (natural monument). It is town-designated by Zaō.
The main hall (honden) of Hiyoshi Shrine, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is town-designated by Zaō, designated on 2025-11-10. Located at Ōaza Hirasawa aza Sanbontsuki 79.
A cluster of potholes (ōketsu) at Kamakurazawa, classified as a monument (natural monument). It is town-designated by Zaō, designated on 2025-11-10. Located within Ōaza Hirasawa aza Kamakurazawa.

The Enda diatomaceous earth deposit, classified as a monument (natural monument). It is town-designated by Zaō, designated on 2025-11-10. Marked at the Kamakurazawa site (Ōaza Hirasawa aza Nihongi) and the Horinouchi River site (Ōaza Enda aza Toriyama).

The chasenko custom of Tōgatta, classified as a folk cultural property (intangible folk cultural property, manners and customs). It is town-designated by Zaō, designated on 2025-11-10. Maintained by the Tōgatta Chasenko Preservation Society.
